clusters.name: multi_agent_non_llm_rm
clusters.description: Multi-agent coordination for resource management via classical RL/MARL without LLM reasoning.
example_titles: Dependent task offloading for air-ground integrated MEC networks: a multi-agent collaboration approach
UAV-D2D Assisted Multi-Agent Parameter-Sharing for Optimizing Energy Consumption and Task Latency in MEC Task Offloading
Federated Reinforcement Learning-Empowered Task Offloading for Large Models in Vehicular Edge Computing

clusters.name: rag_grounding_memory
clusters.description: Knowledge bases, RAG, knowledge graphs, or semantic frameworks for agent grounding.
example_titles: UnifiedKP: A Unified Network Knowledge Plane for Large Model-Enabled 6G Networks
Zero-Knowledge Proof-Enabled LLM-Driven Fusion of Privacy-Sensitive Heterogeneous Sensor Data in Edge Environments
GenNet: Computing-Efficient Generative AI for Deterministic Transmission Scheduling in 6G Networks

clusters.name: llm_support_feature
clusters.description: LLM as a support component for monitoring, anomaly detection, log analysis, or fine-tuning.
example_titles: A Framework for Gen AI Adoption for Enterprises Leveraging Enterprise Architecture Patterns
Enhancing Multi-Modal RCA in Resource Management Systems via Fine-Tuning of LLMs
Accuracy-Aware MLLM Task Offloading and Resource Allocation in UAV-Assisted Satellite Edge Computing

clusters.name: rl_marl_classical
clusters.description: Classical reinforcement learning for resource management without agentic LLM loop.
example_titles: Dependency-Aware Deployment and Retrieval of Intelligent Microservices in AI-RAN
AI-Assisted Synergetic Orchestration Mechanisms for Autoscaling in Computing Continuum Systems
ECMSA: Dual-Agent Learning-Based Edge Caching with Multi-Strategy Adaptation in Dynamic Environments

clusters.name: other_uncertain
clusters.description: Insufficient thematic signal or boundary violations (domain-specific or non-RM focus).
example_titles: An Intelligent Predictive Resource Management Framework for Cloud-Native Systems Based on Large Language Models
Quality-of-Service Aware LLM Routing for Edge Computing with Multiple Experts
Productive Intelligence: AI and Generative AI for Production Optimization and Predictive Maintenance in Oil and Gas Fields - Case Studies from the USA and India

clusters.name: multi_agent_with_llm
clusters.description: Hybrid multi-agent systems combining agent coordination with LLM grounding or reasoning.
example_titles: CoMaTS: A Collaborative Multi-Agent Framework for Edge Task Scheduling
A Human-in-Multi-Agent-Loop Intent Refinement Method for Task Offloading in Mobile Edge Computing
Adaptive GPU Resource Allocation for Multi-Agent Collaborative Reasoning in Serverless Environments

clusters.name: devops_automation_tooling
clusters.description: Container orchestration, Kubernetes, IaC tooling, LLM-driven DevOps (non-RM focus).
example_titles: Automated Bug Discovery in Cloud Infrastructure-as-Code Updates with LLM Agents
Multi-Agent Stateless Orchestration for Distributed Data Pipelines Implementation
Prompt-Driven Container Orchestration

clusters.name: network_telecom_specific
clusters.description: 5G/6G/telecom-focused; potentially out of scope as not core resource management.
example_titles: Optimizing Remote Medical Services with AloT: Integration of Large Language Models and 6G Edge Computing
QoE-Aware Service Provision for Mobile AR Rendering: An Agent-Driven Approach
Generative AI-Assisted Mobile-Edge Computation Offloading in Digital-Twin-Enabled IIoT

clusters.name: agentic_ai_core_rm
clusters.description: LLM-based agent explicitly for resource management decisions (placement, migration, scheduling).
example_titles: Adaptive AI Agent Placement and Migration in Edge Intelligence Systems
AutoHMA-LLM: Efficient Task Coordination and Execution in Heterogeneous Multi-Agent Systems Using Hybrid Large Language Models

clusters.name: generative_ai_inference
clusters.description: Generative AI for inference, AIGC, diffusion model optimization on edge.
example_titles: Optimizing AIGC Services by Prompt Engineering and Edge Computing: A Generative Diffusion Model-Based Contract Theory Approach
